summary: The client requires the development of a comprehensive food delivery application platform.
explicit_requirements: Restaurant onboarding (process for restaurants to join the platform)
Ratings and reviews system (for restaurants and potentially drivers)
assumptions: The app is expected to include distinct interfaces for customers, restaurants, and potentially delivery drivers.
Payment functionality will require integration with a payment gateway.
Live delivery tracking will involve location services and mapping integration.
The platform will support multiple restaurants and multiple end-users.
An underlying backend system is implied to manage all data and logic.
missing_information: Target platforms (e.g., iOS, Android, Web) and their specific requirements.
Detailed feature breakdown for each core module (e.g., user profiles, menu management, order management, payment methods, admin reporting).
Specific payment gateways or methods to be integrated.
Geographical scope or target market for the application.
Monetization strategy (e.g., commission-based, subscription, delivery fees).
Requirements for driver management (e.g., separate driver app, driver onboarding, driver tracking).
Non-functional requirements (e.g., performance, security, scalability, accessibility).
Timeline and budget expectations.
Specific compliance requirements (e.g., data privacy regulations).
Branding guidelines or design preferences.

modules.description: Manages all user types (customers, restaurants, drivers, admins), their accounts, authentication, and profiles.
Handles restaurant-specific functionalities, including menu management, availability, and order reception.
features: Menu Creation and Management (add, edit, delete items, categories)

modules.description: Manages the entire lifecycle of an order, from placement by the customer to delivery confirmation.
features: Customer Order Placement (browsing restaurants, adding items to cart)
Order Confirmation & Status Updates (placed, accepted, prepared, out for delivery, delivered)
Order History & Details (customer, restaurant, admin view)

modules.description: Integrates with payment gateways to process secure transactions for orders and manage refunds.
features: Secure Transaction Processing (credit/debit cards, digital wallets)

modules.description: Manages delivery drivers, assigns orders, and facilitates real-time tracking of deliveries.
features: Live Delivery Tracking (for customers, restaurants, admin)
Driver App Interface (order details, navigation, status updates)

modules.description: Enables users to rate and review restaurants and potentially delivery drivers, fostering quality feedback.
features: Submit Ratings & Reviews for Drivers (optional, but good practice)

modules.description: Handles various types of notifications to users, restaurants, and drivers via push, email, or SMS.
Provides a comprehensive interface for platform administrators to manage users, restaurants, orders, and monitor system performance.
features: Restaurant Management (approve onboarding, manage details, suspend)
Driver Management (approve onboarding, manage details, suspend)
Reporting & Analytics (sales, popular items, delivery performance)

summary: The client requires the development of a comprehensive e-commerce web platform.
assumptions: The platform will require a database to store product, user, order, and seller data.
Payment gateway integration will be necessary for the checkout process.
User authentication and authorization will be implemented for various roles (e.g., buyers, sellers, administrators).
The platform will be accessible via standard web browsers.
Basic security measures will be implemented to protect user and transaction data.
missing_information: Target audience and business model (e.g., B2B, B2C, C2C)?
Specific budget and timeline for the project?
Preferred technology stack or framework?
Detailed UI/UX design requirements and branding guidelines?
Specific analytics metrics and reporting needs?
Requirements for shipping, taxes, and return/refund processes?
Any third-party integrations (e.g., ERP, CRM, marketing automation, existing inventory systems)?
Scalability and performance requirements (e.g., number of concurrent users, transactions per second)?
Maintenance, hosting, and support plan requirements post-launch?
Localization and multilingual support requirements?
Mobile responsiveness or dedicated mobile application requirements?

modules.description: Handles user registration, login, profile management, and role-based access control for different user types (buyers, sellers, administrators).
Manages product listings, categories, product details, and provides comprehensive search and filtering capabilities for buyers.
dependencies: User & Authentication Management (for seller product submission)

modules.description: Allows users to add, remove, and update items in their shopping cart before proceeding to checkout.
features: Cart Persistence (e.g., across sessions for logged-in users)

modules.description: Manages the entire checkout process, from order creation to final placement, and provides order tracking functionality for buyers.
features: Order Tracking (status updates, shipping information integration - future)

modules.description: Integrates with external payment gateways to securely process transactions during the checkout phase.
Provides a dedicated interface and functionalities for sellers to manage their products, inventory, and view their orders.
A back-office module for platform administrators to manage users, sellers, categories, orders, and overall platform settings.
Generates reports and dashboards to provide insights into platform performance, sales, user behavior, and seller activities.

assumptions: The client requires a new digital system to manage core hospital operations.
The application will handle sensitive patient health information and must ensure high data privacy and security.

summary: Develop an enterprise operations platform for a logistics company.
The platform will cover core logistics operations, administrative functions, and technical infrastructure requirements.
assumptions: The platform will be a web-based application, potentially with native mobile components for field access.
The logistics company operates in multiple regions, necessitating region-wise reporting and potentially multi-language support (though not explicitly stated).
The system needs to handle a significant volume of transactions and data given the 'enterprise' and 'high concurrency' requirements.
API integrations will likely be with various internal and external systems relevant to logistics (e.g., carriers, payment gateways, ERPs).
missing_information: Specific regions/countries of operation and associated regulatory requirements.
Target user groups and estimated number of users.
Existing systems landscape and specific integration partners.
Detailed non-functional requirements (e.g., performance metrics, RTO/RPO for disaster recovery).
Budget, timeline, and project phases breakdown.
User experience (UX) and user interface (UI) design preferences.
Data migration strategy from existing systems (if any).
Specific security compliance standards beyond SSO and configurable permissions.
Maintenance and support requirements post-launch.

responsibilities: Implement core backend services (APIs, authentication, reporting logic)
Set up initial DevOps, CI/CD pipelines, and infrastructure
Provide technical guidance and ensure code quality
Develop the interactive analytics dashboard UI
Implement data visualizations and charting components
Integrate with backend APIs and manage frontend state
Ensure responsive design and optimal frontend performance
Conduct user research and define user flows for the dashboard
Create wireframes, prototypes, and high-fidelity mockups
Design intuitive dashboard layouts and data presentation
Ensure a seamless and effective user experience

team.notes: This lean starter team focuses on core technical execution and critical user experience. Product management responsibilities will likely be shared among founders and the Tech Lead initially to maintain agility.

frontend.notes: Next.js provides an excellent developer experience, server-side rendering (SSR), and API routes, enabling a full-stack approach from a single codebase. Tailwind CSS facilitates rapid and consistent UI development. Recharts or Nivo offer robust and flexible data visualization components for interactive dashboards.

backend.authentication: JWT (NextAuth.js if integrated with Next.js or Passport.js)
backend.notes: NestJS provides a robust, modular, and scalable architecture built with TypeScript, promoting maintainability and enterprise readiness. JSON Web Tokens (JWT) enable secure, stateless authentication. Node.js's non-blocking I/O is well-suited for API services.

database.notes: PostgreSQL is a powerful, reliable relational database for user data, configurations, and core application data. TimescaleDB, as a PostgreSQL extension, provides efficient storage and querying capabilities for time-series analytics data. Redis is used for high-performance caching and session management.

infrastructure_devops.deployment: AWS ECS Fargate (for backend), Vercel (for Next.js frontend)
infrastructure_devops.monitoring_logging: AWS CloudWatch, Prometheus/Grafana (for deeper insights later)
infrastructure_devops.notes: AWS offers a comprehensive suite of services for scalability, reliability, and security. Docker ensures consistent environments across development and production. GitHub Actions automates CI/CD workflows. ECS Fargate provides serverless container orchestration, simplifying deployment for the backend, while Vercel is ideal for Next.js applications, offering seamless deployment and global CDN.

stack.notes: This stack leverages modern, widely adopted technologies, ensuring good community support, ample talent availability, and a balance between rapid development and future scalability for a SaaS analytics product.

summary: Development of a comprehensive food delivery mobile application.

Scope of the ratings system (e.g., rating restaurants, drivers, specific dishes).
Detailed requirements for the admin dashboard (e.g., analytics, content management, promotions, dispute resolution).
Geographical scope of the service.

Any specific branding guidelines or UI/UX preferences.

summary: Development of a comprehensive, end-to-end enterprise operations platform for the logistics industry.

assumptions: The platform will be cloud-based to support mobile access and multi-regional reporting.

The system will need to interface with external maps/GPS providers for route optimization.

summary: Development of a high-concurrency enterprise logistics operations platform designed to manage the end-to-end lifecycle of shipments, warehousing, and vendor relations.
The system will integrate advanced planning tools, financial workflows, and real-time monitoring with a focus on scalability and security.
details.project_type: Logistics Management System (LMS) / Enterprise Resource Planning (ERP)
explicit_requirements: Shipment planning and route optimization capabilities
Warehouse operations and vendor management modules
Security features: Audit logs, Single Sign-On (SSO), and Role-Based Access Control (RBAC)
API integrations and document upload functionality
Operational monitoring: SLA tracking and automated alerting
Non-functional: High concurrency support, disaster recovery, and data retention policies
assumptions: The system will be cloud-hosted to facilitate disaster recovery and high availability requirements.
Mobile access requires cross-platform compatibility (iOS and Android).
API integrations include external telematics, GPS providers, and existing accounting software.
The phased rollout will be organized by either geographical region or specific functional modules.
missing_information: Specific volume metrics for 'high concurrency' (e.g., peak transactions per second).

Regulatory compliance requirements (e.g., GDPR, local transport authority standards).

Specific languages or localizations required for regional reporting.
